task_completion.description: Create two vector layer inputs named 'Airports' and 'Urban Areas' in the QGIS Model Designer.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The QGIS Model Designer window should be open, displaying two yellow 'Vector Layer' input boxes named 'Airports' and 'Urban Areas'.

task_completion.description: Add a Buffer algorithm to the model with a 0.05-degree distance and 5 segments, applied to the Airports input.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A 'Buffer' algorithm box in the Model Designer connected to the 'Airports' input. The configuration dialog or box details should show a distance of '0.05' and segments set to '5'.

task_completion.description: Add an Intersection algorithm to the model, combining the buffered airports and the urban areas.
task_completion.visual_evidence: An 'Intersection' algorithm box in the Model Designer. It should have connection lines coming from both the 'Buffer' algorithm's output and the 'Urban Areas' input.

task_completion.description: Name the final output 'Affected Urban Areas' and save the model as airport_impact.model3.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The output of the Intersection algorithm should be labeled 'Affected Urban Areas'. The Model Designer window title or a save dialog should display the filename 'airport_impact.model3' in the /home/ga/GIS_Data/models/ directory.

task_completion.description: Execute the model using the specified Natural Earth shapefiles.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The model execution dialog or processing log showing the model running with '/home/ga/GIS_Data/source/ne_10m_airports.shp' and '/home/ga/GIS_Data/source/ne_10m_urban_areas.shp' selected as the input layers.

task_completion.description: Export the resulting layer to a GeoJSON file named urban_noise_zones.geojson.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A 'Save Vector Layer as...' dialog showing the format as GeoJSON and the file path as '/home/ga/GIS_Data/exports/urban_noise_zones.geojson', or a file manager/terminal showing this file successfully created in the exports folder.

integrity.description: The agent used the QGIS Graphical Modeler to build the model rather than writing the .model3 file manually in a text editor.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show interaction with the QGIS Model Designer interface (dragging inputs/algorithms, connecting boxes).

integrity.description: The agent executed the model and exported the data using QGIS, not via terminal commands.
integrity.visual_evidence: The model run dialog and the 'Save Vector Layer as' dialog in QGIS should be visible, rather than terminal windows running ogr2ogr or gdal commands.

integrity.description: The output file is a genuine result of the geoprocessing operation, not a dummy file.
integrity.visual_evidence: The resulting 'Affected Urban Areas' layer should be visible in the QGIS Layers panel or map canvas, showing actual polygon geometries representing the intersected noise zones.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The task uses real Natural Earth datasets (ne_10m_airports and ne_10m_urban_areas). The buffer distance of 0.05 degrees is approximately 5.5 km at the equator, which is appropriate for noise pollution analysis. The 'segments' parameter (set to 5) is a standard QGIS buffer setting that controls the smoothness of the quarter-circle approximation. QGIS 3.x models are correctly saved with the '.model3' extension. GeoJSON is a standard, natively supported export format in QGIS. Both Buffer and Intersection are standard vector geoprocessing algorithms in QGIS.
